headline: Cultural Appropriation Debate Erupts Over 78 Batik Design Patents
context: Malaysian designers filed complaints after 78 traditional batik patterns were patented by foreign companies in 2024, reigniting debates over cultural ownership and intellectual property.

cards.big: 78 traditional Malaysian batik designs were patented by foreign companies.
cards.sub: Malaysian artisans have no intellectual property framework to protect communal cultural heritage.

cards.big: Malaysia's Intellectual Property Corporation registers individual patents only.
cards.sub: Communal cultural property — shared by communities — has no legal protection mechanism.

cards.big: Indonesia trademarked 500 batik motifs under UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.
cards.sub: Malaysia has not undertaken equivalent registration despite shared batik heritage.

cards.big: The Malaysian batik industry is worth RM340M but only 12% is exported.
cards.sub: Without IP protection, design copying reduces export competitiveness.

cards.big: When others patent your heritage and you cannot stop them, the failure is not cultural — it is legal.
Malaysia needs a communal cultural property framework that protects shared heritage without restricting creative innovation. The current IP system was not designed for communal ownership.
